extracted.brandBackground: Performance-based music schools with a rock music program.

cohorts.label: Company-Owned Designated Schools Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: Average annual total sales of 46 company-owned schools open the full 12 months of 2025.

cohorts.label: Franchised Designated Schools Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: Average annual total sales of 243 franchised schools open the full 12 months of 2025.

cohorts.label: Company-Owned Designated Schools Net Operating Income
cohorts.basis: Average annual net operating income of 46 company-owned schools open the full 12 months of 2025.

item19.notes: Total Sales means all revenue generated at, from, or in connection with the operation of the School.

lineItems.notes: Non-refundable fee for a single school.
Estimated pre-opening rent outlays and security deposits.
Renovation and construction costs net of tenant improvement allowances.
Basic furniture, artwork, wall coverings, and sound panels.
Music equipment, office equipment, and telephones.
Access control system and security cameras.
Travel, lodging, and meals for final week of training.
Grand opening marketing program and Kick It Open Kit.
Branded merchandise and accessories for approximately three months.
Computer hardware, POS, and live streaming equipment.
Zoning, business licenses, and third-party permitting service.
25% of annual premium for general liability and worker's compensation.
Deposits for gas, electricity, telephone, and water.
Working capital for the first three months of operation.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $800 per violation, up to $2,000 per violation plus actual costs.

hiddenCosts.name: Step-In Rights Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 3% of Gross Sales during the period of management plus direct costs.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by arbitration or litigation in Massachusetts, which may be more costly.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Spouses must sign personal guarantees making them personally liable for all franchise obligations.

operationalRisks.title: No Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees do not receive an exclusive territory and may face competition from other outlets or channels.

extracted.conceptRationale: Performance-based music school primarily serving school-age children.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires a General Manager, Music Director, and multiple music instructors to operate the school and teach students.

riskReasons: A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $567,075 mid-point build-out, there is a $317,075 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $52,235/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: Performance-based music school primarily serving school-age children.

benchmark.label: Education / childcare
benchmark.dominantRisk: Regulated staff ratios and enrollment/utilization. Labor dominates and ratios cap how lean you can run.
considerations: Labor is the dominant line at ~40–55% and is floored by regulated student/staff ratios.
Validate enrollment ramp and capacity-utilization curves with Item 20 operators.
Confirm licensing, facility, and compliance costs specific to your state — not in the FDD.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 6.6–9 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Education / childcare” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 10–18% category band.
Estimated at the 5–12% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 40–55% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so School of Rock, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
